properties.remark: MOISTURE LOCKED UP IN MOUNTAIN SNOW BEGAN TO MELT IN LAMOILLE CANYON FROM TEMPERATURES IN THE 60S AND 70S. THIS CAUSED LARGE ROCKS AND BOULDERS TO FALL ONTO AND CLOSE USFS-660 FROM THE POWERHOUSE GROUP PICNIC AREA TO THOMAS CANYON CAMPGROUND.

Heavy rain from thunderstorms produced localized flooding at the intersections of 5th and Fir Streets. Photos from the public show water 3-6 inches deep over the roadway.
